creator: pen@lysator.liu.se
text:
Some data structure in the kernel apparently is too small...

This (similar) warnings pop up on both our Dell and HP servers with SAS drives attached to SAS HBA controllers.

WARNING: Unable to alias gpt/efiboot0 to enc@n500056b3843283fd/type@0/slot@d/elmdesc@Drive_Slot_12/p1/gpt/efiboot0 - path too long
WARNING: Unable to alias msdosfs/EFISYS to enc@n500056b3843283fd/type@0/slot@d/elmdesc@Drive_Slot_12/p1/msdosfs/EFISYS - path too long
WARNING: Unable to alias gpt/gptboot0 to enc@n500056b3843283fd/type@0/slot@d/elmdesc@Drive_Slot_12/p2/gpt/gptboot0 - path too long
WARNING: Unable to alias gpt/efiboot1 to enc@n500056b3843283fd/type@0/slot@e/elmdesc@Drive_Slot_13/p1/gpt/efiboot1 - path too long
WARNING: Unable to alias gpt/gptboot1 to enc@n500056b3843283fd/type@0/slot@e/elmdesc@Drive_Slot_13/p2/gpt/gptboot1 - path too long

Now this isn't a big issue for us but it's still annoying with these warnings :-)
